title: tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. verdickii
intro: Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. verdickii is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential in managing diabetes and hypertension. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia aurantiaca subsp. verdickii often involve drying or powdering the leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
